techniques.evidence_quote: One of the documents seen by BleepingComputer includes email addresses and Windows Active Directory information for over 77,000 Uber employees.
unauthorized access to our systems by a malicious third party

Uber suffers new data breach after attack on vendor...Uber referred us to a Teqtivity data breach notification...which explains that a threat actor gained access to a Teqtivity AWS backup server that stores data for its customers.
The third party was able to gain access to our Teqtivity AWS backup server that housed Teqtivity code and data files related to Teqtivity customers.
a threat actor named 'UberLeaks' began leaking data they claimed was stolen from Uber and Uber Eats on a hacking forum

attack_chain.description: Attacker targeted Teqtivity, a trusted third-party IT asset management vendor used by Uber, to gain indirect access to Uber's data without directly compromising Uber's own infrastructure.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: We believe these files are related to an incident at a third-party vendor and are unrelated to our security incident in September.

attack_chain.description: Attacker gained unauthorized access to Teqtivity's systems through an unknown initial access vector (root cause undisclosed); compromise of the Teqtivity environment preceded access to the AWS backup server.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: unauthorized access to our systems by a malicious third party

attack_chain.description: Attacker accessed Teqtivity's AWS backup server, which housed source code and customer data files including device and user information for Teqtivity customers such as Uber.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The third party was able to gain access to our Teqtivity AWS backup server that housed Teqtivity code and data files related to Teqtivity customers.

attack_chain.description: Attacker exfiltrated stolen data including source code archives, IT asset management reports, Windows domain login names, email addresses, and corporate information from the AWS backup server.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: BleepingComputer has been told that the newly leaked data consists of source code, IT asset management reports, data destruction reports, Windows domain login names and email addresses, and other corporate information.

attack_chain.description: Attacker published the stolen data on a public hacking forum under the alias 'UberLeaks', leaking email addresses and Windows Active Directory information for over 77,000 Uber employees, enabling future targeted phishing attacks.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a threat actor named 'UberLeaks' began leaking data they claimed was stolen from Uber and Uber Eats on a hacking forum...One of the documents seen by BleepingComputer includes email addresses and Windows Active Directory information for over 77,000 Uber employees.

initial_access_vector: Unknown — Teqtivity disclosed unauthorized third-party access to their systems but did not publicly reveal the root cause or specific initial access method used.
